en.extract:
The Siamese fireback (Lophura diardi), also known as Diard's fireback, is a fairly large, approximately 80 cm (31 in) long, pheasant. The male has a grey plumage with an extensive facial caruncle, crimson legs and feet, ornamental black crest feathers, reddish brown iris and long curved blackish tail. The female is a brown bird with blackish wings and tail feathers.

The Siamese fireback is distributed to the lowland and evergreen forests of Cambodia, Laos, Thailand, and Vietnam in Southeast Asia. However, in June 2025, the bird was spotted naturally in the forest area of Ranikhet, a popular tourist destination in Uttarakhand, India. This species is also designated as Thailand's national bird. The female usually lays between four and eight rosy eggs.


Naming
The scientific name commemorates the French naturalist Pierre-Médard Diard.
This species of pheasant has a common name in Thai Kai Fah Phaya Lo (Thai: ไก่ฟ้าพญาลอ; Lord Lo's pheasant), according to Thai folk literature, Lilit Phra Lo where Phra Lo, the protagonist, is charmed by following the pheasant until he meets Phra Phuean and Phra Phaeng, the two sisters and later his lovers.


Status
Due to habitat loss and over-hunting in some areas, the Siamese fireback was evaluated as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List; however, it is now Least Concern.


Gallery


See also

The '''Siamese fireback''' ('''''Lophura diardi'''''), also known as '''Diard's fireback''', is a fairly large, approximately {{Convert|80|cm|in|abbr=on}} long, [[pheasant]]. The male has a grey plumage with an extensive facial [[caruncle (bird anatomy)|caruncle]], crimson legs and feet, ornamental black crest feathers, reddish brown [[Iris (anatomy)|iris]] and long curved blackish tail. The female is a brown bird with blackish wings and tail feathers.
[[File:Lophura diardi -Florida, USA -captive-8a (2).jpg|thumb|right|Head]]
[[File:Lophura diardi MHNT.jpg|thumb|''Lophura diardi'']]
The Siamese fireback is distributed to the lowland and evergreen forests of [[Cambodia]], [[Laos]], [[Thailand]], and [[Vietnam]] in [[Southeast Asia]]. However, in June 2025, the bird was spotted naturally in the forest area of [[Ranikhet]], a popular tourist destination in [[Uttarakhand]], [[India]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Sethi |first=Narendra |date=2025-06-10 |title=Avian enthusiasts thrilled as Thailand's national bird spotted for first time in Uttarakhand's Ranikhet |url=https://www.newindianexpress.com/nation/2025/Jun/10/avian-enthusiasts-thrilled-as-thailands-national-bird-spotted-for-first-time-in-uttarakhands-ranikhet |access-date=2025-06-17 |website=The New Indian Express |language=en}}</ref> This species is also designated as Thailand's [[national bird]]. The female usually lays between four and eight rosy eggs.

==Naming==

The scientific name commemorates the [[France|French]] [[natural history|naturalist]] [[Pierre-Médard Diard]].

This species of pheasant has a common name in Thai ''Kai Fah Phaya Lo'' ({{langx|th|ไก่ฟ้าพญาลอ}}; Lord Lo's pheasant), according to Thai folk literature, ''[[Lilit Phra Lo]]'' where Phra Lo, the protagonist, is charmed by following the pheasant until he meets Phra Phuean and Phra Phaeng, the two sisters and later his lovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.xn--12c4dtc9cvah.com/%E0%B9%84%E0%B8%81%E0%B9%88%E0%B8%9F%E0%B9%89%E0%B8%B2%E0%B9%84%E0%B8%97%E0%B8%A2_page2|title=ไก่ฟ้าในประเทศไทย|trans-title=Pheasants of Thailand|language=thai|work=ไก่ฟ้า.com}}</ref>

==Status==
Due to habitat loss and over-hunting in some areas, the Siamese fireback was evaluated as Near Threatened on the [[IUCN Red List]]; however, it is now Least Concern.<ref name="iucn status 11 November 2021" />

zh.extract:
戴氏火背鹇（學名：Lophura diardi），又名泰國火背鷴，是一種中等大小，約有80厘米長的雉。雄雞呈灰色，大部份面部沒有羽毛及呈紅色，腳呈深紅色，冠上羽毛呈黑色，虹膜呈紅褐色，尾巴長而彎曲及呈黑色。雌雞呈褐色，雙翼及尾羽黑色。
泰國火背鷴分佈在柬埔寨、老撾、泰國及越南的低地及常綠林。它們是泰國的國鳥。雌雞每次會生4-8隻蛋，蛋呈紅色。
泰國火背鷴的學名是為紀念法國自然學家Pierre-Médard Diard。
由於不斷失去棲息地及過度獵殺，世界自然保護聯盟已將它們列為近危。
